title: Laundry Care Symbols: Wash, Dry & Iron Symbol Meanings Chart
meta_description: Laundry care symbols and their meanings — wash, bleach, dry, and iron care icons in copy-paste Unicode. Click any symbol to copy it for labels, notes, and posts.
hero_tagline: Wash, bleach, dry, and iron care icons you can copy and paste. Click any symbol to copy it instantly.
intro: The official ISO 3758 laundry care symbols printed on clothing tags are not encoded as standalone Unicode text characters, so this reference gathers the closest Unicode equivalents — emoji and geometric shapes — grouped by what each care instruction means: washing and water, bleaching, drying, ironing heat levels, and “do not” prohibitions. Every tile copies a single character you can paste into care labels, packing lists, notes, and posts. Use the Unicode name shown beside each symbol so readers know exactly what it stands for.

sections.label: Washing
sections.intro: Closest Unicode stand-ins for the wash-tub care instruction — water, detergent, and machine-wash cues.

sections.label: Bleaching
sections.intro: The bleach care symbol is a triangle; an empty triangle means bleach is allowed and a crossed one means do not bleach.

sections.label: Drying
sections.intro: Sun, circle, and square shapes echo the tumble-dry and line-dry care icons.

sections.label: Ironing
sections.intro: Iron care symbols use one, two, or three dots for low, medium, and high heat — these Unicode dot characters mirror that scale.

sections.label: Do Not
sections.intro: On a care tag, a cross through any symbol means do not do it. Pair these with the symbols above to signal do not wash, bleach, dry, or iron.

related.title: Hazard & Warning Symbols
related.desc: Warning triangles, caution signs, and hazard symbols for safety labels.

related.title: Recycle & Environment Symbols
related.desc: Recycling arrows and eco symbols for packaging and care labels.

related.title: Geometric Symbols
related.desc: Triangles, squares, circles, and other shapes used in care iconography.
